cueCards.topic: Describe a skill you would like to learn
bullets: What the skill is
Why you want to learn it
How you would go about learning it
How this skill would help you in the future
followUpQuestions: What skills are most valuable in today's job market?
Do you think it is harder to learn new skills as you get older?
How has technology changed the way people learn new skills?

cueCards.topic: Describe a place in your city you like to visit
bullets: Where this place is
What you can do there
Who you usually go there with
Why you enjoy visiting this place
followUpQuestions: How important is it for cities to have public spaces?
Do you think young people and older people enjoy the same kinds of places?
How have public spaces in cities changed over the years?

cueCards.topic: Describe a time you helped someone
bullets: Who you helped and what the situation was
What you did to help them
How the person reacted
How you felt about helping
followUpQuestions: Do you think people today are less willing to help others than in the past?
What can governments do to encourage people to volunteer?
Is it better to help people you know personally, or to help strangers?

cueCards.topic: Describe a book or film that had a strong effect on you
bullets: What the book or film was about
When and where you first read or saw it
What made it memorable
How it changed your thinking or feelings
followUpQuestions: Do you think reading books is more beneficial than watching films?
How has the internet changed the way people access stories and entertainment?
Why do you think some stories remain popular for many generations?

cueCards.topic: Describe a goal you would like to achieve in the next five years
bullets: What the goal is
Why you want to achieve it
What steps you plan to take
How achieving this goal would change your life
followUpQuestions: Do you think it is important to set goals in life?
Is it better to have short-term or long-term goals?
How do people in your country typically plan for their future?

bullets: Where your hometown is located
What it looks like and what it is known for
What you like most about it
How your hometown has changed over the years
followUpQuestions: What are the advantages of growing up in a small town compared to a big city?
Do you think people feel a strong connection to their hometowns?
How does where someone grows up shape their personality and outlook?

cueCards.topic: Describe a traditional dish from your country
bullets: What the dish is called and what it looks like
What the main ingredients are
When people usually eat it
Why this dish is important in your culture
followUpQuestions: How important is food in bringing people together in your culture?
Do you think traditional foods are becoming less popular among young people?
How has globalisation affected the food culture in your country?

cueCards.topic: Describe a person from your youth who had a great influence on you
bullets: Where you met this person
What relationship this person was to you
What was special about them
How they influenced you so much
followUpQuestions: Who do you think has more influence on children — parents or teachers?
Do you think role models are important for young people?
How have the kinds of people young people look up to changed over time?

cueCards.topic: Describe a historical place you have visited or would like to visit
bullets: Where this place is
What happened there historically
What the place looks like today
Why you find it interesting or why you would like to visit it
followUpQuestions: How important is it for countries to preserve their historical sites?
Do young people today have enough interest in history?
How can governments encourage more people to visit historical places?

cueCards.topic: Describe a competition you have entered
bullets: What the competition was
How you prepared for it
What happened during the competition
What you learned from the experience
followUpQuestions: Do you think competition is good for people's development?
Are competitive people more successful in life?
How do attitudes towards winning and losing vary between different cultures?

cueCards.topic: Describe a time when you had to make an important decision
bullets: What decision you had to make
What factors you considered
What decision you finally made
How you felt about the outcome
followUpQuestions: Do you think people make better decisions as they get older?
Should young people be given more freedom to make their own decisions?
How do you think technology has changed the way people make decisions?

cueCards.topic: Describe a subject you enjoyed studying at school
bullets: What the subject was
Who taught it to you
What you found interesting about it
How studying this subject has helped you since
followUpQuestions: Do you think the school curriculum in your country prepares students well for the future?
Should students have more choice about what subjects they study?
How has the role of teachers changed with the rise of the internet?

cueCards.topic: Describe a time when you visited a city that impressed you
bullets: Which city you visited and when
Why you went there
What you saw and did there
Why this city made such an impression on you
followUpQuestions: What makes a city attractive to tourists?
Do you think mass tourism is damaging to cities?
How might cities change in the future to deal with growing populations?

cueCards.topic: Describe a time when you used a foreign language
bullets: What language it was and where you used it
Why you needed to use it
How well you were able to communicate
How you felt about using a foreign language
followUpQuestions: How important is it to learn a second language today?
Do you think children learn languages more easily than adults?
What impact does speaking multiple languages have on a person's career opportunities?

cueCards.topic: Describe a type of sport or physical activity you enjoy
bullets: What the sport or activity is
How often you do it and where
Who you do it with
Why you enjoy it and what benefits it brings you
followUpQuestions: Why do you think some people find it hard to exercise regularly?
Should governments do more to encourage people to be physically active?
How has the way people exercise changed over the past 20 years?
